discussion_comments.comment_body: Fixed — added BaseResp to the response struct and check status_code != 0 before accepting vectors.
Addressed in 553e9d39b - added a `len(item.Embedding) == 0` fast-fail before slice conversion, plus `TestDeepSeekEncode_RejectsEmptyEmbedding` to cover the path.

Done. Two things the fix enforces:

- Out-of-range index — if the API returns a result with an index outside `[0, len(texts))`, we now return an error immediately instead of silently dropping it. This catches malformed API responses.
- Count mismatch — after the loop, if `len(results) != len(texts)`, we error. Since we pass `top_n = len(texts)`, the API is expected to return a score for every document; fewer results means at least one document got a silent 0.0 which is indistinguishable from a genuine zero-relevance score.
Hi, good call! Fixed in commit 870e889, an out-of-range index now returns a clear error instead of silently skipping the entry.
